description_long: This stone obelisk features a square base with a raised platform, supporting a central block adorned with intricate carvings. Each side of the central block displays a seated Buddha figure in relief, framed by a rectangular border. The lower section of the obelisk is decorated with detailed bas-relief panels depicting natural motifs such as birds, flowers, and flowing water. The stone surface is matte and slightly rough, with visible weathering and subtle color variations in gray tones. The structure is solid and heavy, likely carved from a single block or assembled from large stone pieces. The overall design suggests a commemorative or religious function, suitable for placement in parks, plazas, or temple courtyards.
description: A solid stone obelisk with a square base, ornate relief carvings of Buddha figures, and decorative panels featuring natural motifs.
description_view_0: Front view shows a seated Buddha relief on the central block and a lower panel with birds and floral motifs.
description_view_1: This side displays another Buddha relief above a panel with a flowing water scene and floral decorations.
description_view_2: The rear view features a Buddha relief and a less detailed lower panel, with the stone appearing lighter due to lighting.
description_view_3: This side presents a Buddha relief above a panel with a dynamic water motif and floral carvings along the base.
attribute_ornamentation: Ornamentation includes detailed bas-relief carvings of seated Buddha figures on each side of the central block, and decorative panels with birds, flowers, and flowing water motifs on the lower section and base.
attribute_base_design: The base is a broad, square platform with a raised step, featuring carved floral motifs along the sides and a central decorative panel on each face. The structure is solid and appears to be made from a single or several large stone blocks.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is flat and open, forming a square enclosure with no pointed or pyramidal top, and is bordered by the upper edges of the central block.
